addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: idempotent-canonical-invests (₢BMAAG) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Theurge robustness fix; mostly mechanical, with one empirical timing check.
  
  ## Problem
  
  canonical-invest reruns against a standing depot fail: `rbgg_invest_retriever`
  and `rbgg_invest_director` are create-only and return HTTP 409 when the SA
  already exists from a prior run. `governor_mantle` does NOT have this problem —
  it deletes existing `governor-*` SAs before recreating ("Clean up existing
  governor-* service accounts (404-tolerant)"). Skirmish is designed to rerun
  against a standing depot, so this breaks every run after the first.
  
  ## Fix
  
  Give the retriever/director invests the same self-heal the mantle already has: a
  delete-first-tolerate-404 prelude inside the invest operation. The
  GovernorDivests verbs (`rbw-arD` / `rbw-adD`, zero-arg by-role) already exist to
  do the deletion. Preferred over a sequence-level divest step in the
  canonical-invest fixture, because folding it into the operation also makes
  standalone single-case reruns idempotent and co-locates cleanup with creation,
  matching the governor precedent.
  
  ## Caveat to verify
  
  Tight delete→recreate timing. SA deletion has propagation latency and GCP can
  balk at recreating a just-deleted SA name (soft-delete tombstone). Proven this
  session that delete-then-recreate works with ~1 min of intervening delay; an
  immediate divest→invest is tighter. The mantle already carries a "Wait for SA
  propagation" step — the invest self-heal likely needs the same settle. One
  tight-timing rerun confirms.
  
  ## Done
  
  canonical-invest reruns clean against a standing depot — no 409 — both as the
  full skirmish suite and as a standalone single-case rerun.

1d098acd0  ₣BM       RBSRR drift correction (two content errors found while tracing RBRR's authority/runtime ambiguities). (1) Opening line claimed RBRR assignment files are 'found in the operator's local filesystem' — implying personal/isolated/uncommitted — but rbmm_moorings/rbrr.env is tracked; reworded to 'committed to the repository' (authority-neutral; does not codify Director-as-sole-editor, deferred to BS credential reorientation). (2) RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX group rationale claimed it 'enables distinct developers to coexist on the same workstation without name collisions' — wrong: operators live in distinct container runtimes (separate daemons/machines), so that collision class mostly does not exist; the prefix's real job is disambiguating RB-lifecycle-managed runtime resources from OTHER container systems sharing the same runtime, reducing collateral disturbance. Reworded to that. Left line 39 (RBRR_SECRETS_DIR 'outside the source repository') intact — the one genuinely machine-local field, consistent with a committed file naming a per-machine path. Deferred (not wrong, just missing, pending BS): quoin-linking bare 'operator'->{at_operator} (possessive-variant risk + legacy quoin being reconsidered), at_operator credential clause, Director-as-authority codification, RBRR field-by-field authority attribution, runtime regime-field home.                                                                                                                                                                                                 
e0d8fb281  ₢BMAAG    Make canonical-invest reruns idempotent via spec-honoring fixture-level divest (option A), not invest auto-heal. Honoring RBSRK/RBSDK intent, invest stays fail-loud: added the existence preflight the code never actually implemented (GET before create -> buc_die 'run divest first to re-key' instead of letting create raw-409). The canonical-invest fixture now runs an explicit divest before invest in the shared rbtdrk_role_invest_impl; 404-tolerant, so canonical-establish on a fresh depot is a clean no-op. New rbgu_poll_until_gone (inverse of rbgu_poll_until_ok: polls a GET until HTTP 404, same RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC deadline); zrbgg_divest_role now polls-until-gone after a real delete (skipped on 404-already-absent), absorbing the seconds-scale deletion-propagation race so an immediately-following same-name invest is race-free. WebSearch confirmed the GCP 30-day soft-delete window governs undelete/purge, not recreate -- recreate of a same name is supported (fresh identity), so the only residual is propagation timing, not a tombstone. RBSRD/RBSDD gain a Confirm-Deletion-Propagated step ({rbbc_poll} until 404, skip-on-404). RBGG 409-is-fatal invariant preserved (the preflight is a fail-loud implementation of it, not a carve-out). Theurge builds clean, 114/114 unit tests, shellcheck clean under busc_shellcheckrc. Live skirmish rerun (standing-depot acceptance gate) deferred to operator. Identity-model spec/code drift (singleton-per-role/no-arg vs per-identity/param1) left untouched, flagged for a separate spook.
5df1ac6d5  ₣BM       Fix skirmish suite ordering to mirror the gauntlet: move the three validation fixtures (regime-validation, regime-smoke, dockerfile-hygiene) to AFTER canonical-invest + onboarding-sequence, instead of before. The hoisted-early ordering ran regime-validation's rbrn_all_nameplates against a marshal-zero tree where nameplate hallmarks (RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK) are still empty, which rbrn_validate correctly rejects (required, min-len 1). The gauntlet already encodes the invariant that hallmark-bearing validation runs after the build fixtures populate hallmarks (onboarding-sequence kludges all 5 nameplates incl ccyolo); skirmish had diverged. Verified live: skirmish now advances enrollment-validation (47/47) -> canonical-invest cleanly, where it correctly stops at the depot/org-policy wall (separate concern, captured as BS pace org-affiliated-account-reorientation).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
144c72333  ₣BM       Add Import Discipline section to RCG (after Crate Boilerplate): multi-item use braces one-per-line, alphabetically ordered, trailing comma, for change-proportional diffs (blame precision, fewer merge collisions, deterministic insertion point). Codifies the convention applied twice this session. Note-on-rustfmt records the measured finding: alphabetization is rustfmt's stable reorder_imports default, but imports_layout=Vertical is nightly-only (stable warns and ignores via rustfmt.toml); max_width=1 rejected as a global sledgehammer. Discipline is review-enforced since the project does not run cargo fmt.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
82ba4bbad  ₣BM       Fix rbw-tT: move the build->charge hallmark commit into the fixture layer (the precedent is onboarding's rbtdro_kludge_nameplate, which kludges sentry+bottle and commits each). New kludge-tadmor fixture reuses rbtdro_onboarding_kludge_tadmor_impl via a thin probe-free case (drops the onboarding reliquary-stamp witness probe, which is a sequencing guard not a local-kludge dep). rbq_qualify_tadmor now runs FixtureRun kludge-tadmor (builds BOTH vessels - earlier rbw-tK only built the bottle - and commits the hallmarks) then FixtureRun tadmor (charges against the now-clean nameplate). Avoids both the bash-git-in-qualify smell and the fixture-name==nameplate collision. Manifest const + colophon arm (KLUDGE_SENTRY/BOTTLE) added; registered in RBTDRC_FIXTURES; onboarding manifest import reformatted one-per-line per RCG. Also commits the stale rbrn.env bottle hallmark from the prior failed run so the tree is clean for the kludge assertion. Theurge builds + 114 tests green.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
ab67ebadb  ₣BM       Regenerate tabtarget context (rbw-MG) for the new rbw-tS/rbw-tT colophons added to the zipper — the rbw workbench's qualify_fast preflight gate rejects a stale generated context, so this is required for any rbw-* command to run after the enrollment. Also commit the tadmor bottle kludge hallmark (RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=k260522063922-527879e18) driven into tadmor/rbrn.env by the first rbw-tT run, so the next kludge's clean-tree assertion passes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
527879e18  ₣BM       Fix RCG magic-string violation in rbtdtk_family_stem_value: the test pinned the literal 'canest2' (duplicating RBTDRK_FAMILY_STEM_BASE), so the canest2->canest3 era-bump of the source const left the test falsely failing. Rewrote to assert the composition behavior (rbtdrk_family_stem('xyz') == format!('{}xyz', RBTDRK_FAMILY_STEM_BASE)) tied to the constant, dropping the tautological literal-equality assert. Theurge 114/114 green.

Also commit the tadmor bottle kludge hallmark (RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=k260522063922-527879e18) driven into tadmor/rbrn.env by the first rbw-tT run, so the next kludge's clean-tree assertion passes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
527879e18  ₣BM       Fix RCG magic-string violation in rbtdtk_family_stem_value: the test pinned the literal 'canest2' (duplicating RBTDRK_FAMILY_STEM_BASE), so the canest2->canest3 era-bump of the source const left the test falsely failing. Rewrote to assert the composition behavior (rbtdrk_family_stem('xyz') == format!('{}xyz', RBTDRK_FAMILY_STEM_BASE)) tied to the constant, dropping the tautological literal-equality assert. content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BM
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Regime enums in this project carry their values as bare English words —
6	`conjure`, `bind`, `graft` for `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`; `tether`, `airgap` for
7	`RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`; node-regime modes (`disabled`/`enabled`/`global`/
8	`allowlist`) for the `RBRN_*` family. These bare values appear scattered
9	across bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build steps), and Rust (test
10	fixtures), with no cluster-discoverable marker tying any one value to its
11	enum family. This makes mode renames cross-language hand-search-and-replace
12	work, prone to silent drift.
13	
14	There is one prior in-tree exemplar of the prefixed-value approach:
15	`bubep_linux | bubep_mac | bubep_windows` for `BURN_PLATFORM`. It works but
16	uses a different shape from what this heat adopts; harmonization is part of
17	the sweep.
18	
19	The inventory pace has been completed (see heat log / slate commit
20	messages for depth). This paddock records the resolved scope and the
21	durable findings; line-numbered site lists deliberately live in the
22	discovery recipe, not here.
23	
24	## MCM grounding — "sprue" is `mcm_sprue`, prefix discipline makes it an inlay
25	
26	"Sprue" here is not a coined word — it is the MCM quoin `mcm_sprue`: *a
27	literal wire-level token representing a serialized name — the exact
28	character sequence as it appears in a wire format (a JSON property key, an
29	API field name, a protocol identifier).* Our regime enum values are
30	textbook sprues: `rbnve_conjure` is the verbatim token that appears in
31	`rbrv.env`, in the `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` Cloud Build substitution, in
32	build-info JSON, and in Python/shell comparisons.
33	
34	MCM also states: *a sprue that follows the prefix naming discipline is also
35	an `mcm_inlay`* (prefix-recognized, not lexicon-catalogued). So precisely,
36	the values are *already* sprues even while bare; what this heat adds is the
37	prefix discipline that makes each sprue **also an inlay**. The spec pace
38	(₢BMAAA) should cite `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding rather than
39	inventing rationale.
40	
41	Note on a sibling usage: ₣BK independently uses "sprue" for tabtarget
42	dispatch tokens (`{owner}ml_{launcher-id}`, e.g. `rbml_rbw`) and wrote a
43	local gloss in BCG/CLAUDE.md. That is *also* a valid `mcm_sprue` instance
44	(a literal dispatch-wire token) — not a competing definition, a sibling
45	one. Both heats correctly name wire-level literal tokens "sprue." ₣BK's BCG
46	text would ideally cite `mcm_sprue` rather than read as a freshly-minted
47	concept, but that is ₣BK's documentation concern, not a blocker here.
48	
49	## Convention — locked
50	
51	Pattern: `<proj><n><regime><e>_<value>` where:
52	
53	- `<proj>` — project prefix (`rb` for Recipe Bottle, `bu` for BUK)
54	- `<n>`    — single letter signaling "this is a regime namespace value"
55	             (chosen `n` for "namespace", contrasting `r` which is taken
56	             for the regime-variable family `rbr*`/`bur*`)
57	- `<regime>` — single letter mirroring the regime family
58	             (`v` = vessel, `n` = node, `c` = config, `s` = station,
59	             `e` = environment, etc.)
60	- `<e>`    — single letter signaling "this is an enum value"
61	- `_<value>` — the canonical value name
62	
63	Full target table (all in-scope enums):
64	
65	| Variable                 | Regime | Bare → Sprue                                          |
66	|--------------------------|:------:|-------------------------------------------------------|
67	| RBRV_VESSEL_MODE         | v      | bind/conjure/graft → `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft` |
68	| RBRV_EGRESS_MODE         | v      | tether/airgap → `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`              |
69	| RBRN_ENTRY_MODE          | n      | disabled/enabled → `rbnne_disabled`/`_enabled`        |
70	| RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE     | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
71	| RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
72	| BURN_PLATFORM            | n      | bubep_linux/mac/windows → `bunne_linux`/`_mac`/`_windows` |
73	
74	Family-sharing is a deliberate property, not an accident: vessel-mode and
75	egress-mode share `rbnve_` (both vessel-regime); the three node-mode enums
76	share `rbnne_`, so `rbnne_disabled` is intentionally voiced by ENTRY,
77	DNS, and ACCESS alike. Uniqueness lives in the full quoin + the owning
78	variable, not in the 5-char prefix. A single grep per family returns the
79	whole cluster.
80	
81	## Scope decisions — resolved
82	
83	- **RBRV_VESSEL_MODE + RBRV_EGRESS_MODE** → `rbnve_*`. In.
84	- **RBRN_ENTRY_MODE / UPLINK_DNS_MODE / UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE** → `rbnne_*`.
85	  In. Their values are pure comparison tokens with no second life, so the
86	  prefix is free (no indirection needed).
87	- **BURN_PLATFORM** → harmonize `bubep_*` to `bunne_*` in the same sweep.
88	  Decided harmonize over grandfather, per load-bearing-complexity
89	  discipline — eliminates the two-shape wart rather than freezing it.
90	- **RBRN_RUNTIME (`docker`/`podman`) — EXCLUDED, stays bare.** Its value
91	  is consumed as the literal executable name (`command -v "${RBRN_RUNTIME}"`,
92	  `${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect`). Adopting the convention would require a
93	  permanent value→binary indirection layer at every dispatch site — a
94	  runtime cost paid for grep-discoverability of two values that
95	  `RBRN_RUNTIME` itself already marks. The convention would cost more than
96	  it returns. This is the one regime enum that does not earn the prefix;
97	  the exclusion is deliberate, not an oversight.
98	- **BURE_VERBOSE / BURE_COLOR** — out (bare ints, different problem class;
99	  unchanged from original framing).
100	
101	## Comment / use-site classification — judgment work
102	
103	Bare-word grep on a value yields four classes. The migration paces walk
104	the candidate list and classify each hit:
105	
106	1. **Phase / concept references** — "the conjure phase", "the airgap
107	   build" — refer to the workflow, not the enum value. **Leave alone.**
108	2. **Enum-value references** — equality tests, case arms, JSON literals,
109	   `vessel mode is conjure`, defaults — the actual variable value.
110	   **Migrate.**
111	3. **Identifier embeds** — `RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc`, `rbho_conjure_*`,
112	   vessel sigils `rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap`, test-step labels like
113	   `ordain-conjure` — the bare word is part of an identifier. **Leave
114	   alone.**
115	4. **Coupled derived strings** — the value drives a derived token that
116	   lives in resource-name space and must stay bare even though it is
117	   semantically the enum value. **Leave the derived string bare; migrate
118	   only the dispatch that selects it.** Two confirmed witnesses:
119	   - egress → worker-pool suffixes (`RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_*`, the `-tether`/
120	     `-airgap` fragments in pool paths)
121	   - vessel-mode → hallmark prefixes (`RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_*` = `c`/`b`/`g`)
122	   Because of class 4, even a class-2 case arm is not blind find/replace:
123	   the arm becomes `rbnve_tether)` while the pool string it maps to stays
124	   `-tether`.
125	
126	### Three judgment traps a naive grep misses
127	
128	- **Default-value defaults** — `${z_mode:-conjure}` is class-2 but a
129	  `== "conjure"` grep skips it. The recipe must also grep `:-conjure`,
130	  `:-bind`, etc.
131	- **AsciiDoc wire-value tables read like prose** — a spec table cell
132	  documenting `(conjure, bind, or graft)` as a substitution value is
133	  class-2 (migrate), not class-1 prose. Easy to misclassify and leave
134	  stale.
135	- **Rust noise is almost all class-3** — the onboarding-scenario fixtures
136	  are full of step labels (`ordain-conjure`, `wrest-bind`) and sigil
137	  consts that stay bare. Only the `export RBRV_*_MODE="..."` baseline
138	  strings in the fast-fixture file actually migrate; that pace rebuilds
139	  the Rust crate (`tt/vow-b`).
140	
141	### Noise ranking (classification effort)
142	
143	Counterintuitively `conjure` is the noisiest value (heavy phase/operation
144	prose), then `bind` (English-word noise: bind-mount, IP bind), then
145	`graft`; `tether`/`airgap` are the cleanest (~10 sites each, mostly
146	class-2 or class-4). This is why egress is the cheap pilot and vessel-mode
147	is the heavy classification grind.
148	
149	## Cloud Build detection gap — vessel-mode acceptance gate
150	
151	Vessel-mode's value crosses a substitution boundary
152	(`_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` / `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`) into a **remote execution
153	context** — Cloud Build step shells and Python steps compare against the
154	literal value. The local `regime-validation` net catches the
155	declaration↔Rust-fixture lockstep, but it does **not** exercise the
156	remote-side comparisons. A missed step-shell or Python site fails silently
157	until an actual conjure/graft cloud build runs.
158	
159	**Consequence:** for the vessel-mode pace, "gauntlet green" is not
160	sufficient proof of done. Its acceptance gate is an actual **conjure +
161	graft cloud build** (the full ordain path), not just the fast/crucible
162	suites.
163	
164	## Discovery recipe
165	
166	- `grep -rn 'buv_enum_enroll' Tools/buk/ Tools/rbk/` — authoritative
167	  declarations; cross-walk `buv_gate_enroll` invocations to confirm no
168	  enum missed.
169	- Per value V: `grep -rn '\bV\b' Tools/ tt/` filtered by extension for
170	  code sites; also `grep -rn ':-V\b'` for default-value defaults.
171	- Comment/prose: `grep -rn '#.*\bV\b'` (bash/Python), `'//.*\bV\b'`
172	  (Rust), and scan `Tools/*/vov_veiled/*.adoc` for prose + wire-value
173	  tables.
174	- Substitution boundary: trace `_RBG[AV]_VESSEL_MODE` /
175	  `_RBG[AV]_EGRESS*` through `rbfd_*`/`rbfv_*` step assembly into the
176	  `rbgja*`/`rbgjv*` Python+shell steps.
177	
178	## Posture
179	
180	**Burn-bridges migration.** No backwards compatibility. Depots reform on
181	execution, vessels re-charge, regime files get edited in place. Operator
182	pre-flight: `rbw-MZ` before sweep, all crucibles quenched.
183	
184	**Deep-test stragglers.** `regime-validation`, `dockerfile-hygiene`, and
185	`gauntlet` catch any local bare-value site, because the enum-validation
186	gate rejects a non-prefixed value once the declaration is updated. The
187	remote Cloud Build comparisons are the exception — see the detection gap
188	above.
189	
190	## Heat-shape note — ₣BK relationship (BK coding landed)
191	
192	₣BK (moorings-cutover) swept essentially this heat's file surface
193	(`Tools/rbk/**/*.sh`, `rbtd/src/*.rs`, `RBS*.adoc`) — but for path literals,
194	not enum values. The two heats are **semantically orthogonal** (different
195	concerns, mostly different lines). ₣BK's *coding* has landed (BKAAI
196	wrapped); the prior file-surface-churn concern is now historical — the tree
197	is settled and the re-validation confirmed the enum-value sites survived
198	intact.
199	
200	What remains and matters:
201	- **Start gate:** ₣BK's two-platform gauntlet acceptance is still pending.
202	  Begin this heat only after ₣BK's gauntlet passes on linux + macos and any
203	  cutover bugfixes land — so a known-good cutover baseline exists and every
204	  ₣BM gauntlet signal is unambiguously about enums, not the cutover.
205	- **Residual churn risk:** only late ₣BK bugfixes from its gauntlet runs.
206	  Low; re-grep before editing if BK commits land meanwhile.
207	- The bubep→bunne harmonize touches BUK files (₣BK's other subtree). ₣BK's
208	  BUK code has landed, so no hard coordination remains — just awareness of
209	  any late bugfix churn there.
210	
211	## What done looks like
212	
213	- All in-scope bare enum values replaced with sprue-prefixed values across
214	  bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build), and Rust; `RBRN_RUNTIME` left bare.
215	- `buv_enum_enroll` (and gated `buv_gate_enroll`) declarations updated to
216	  expect the new values; regime files (`rbrv.env`, `rbrn.env`, BURN
217	  profiles) updated in place.
218	- Class-4 derived strings (pool suffixes, hallmark prefixes) left bare;
219	  their selecting dispatch migrated.
220	- `regime-validation` + `dockerfile-hygiene` + `gauntlet` green.
221	- Vessel-mode additionally proven by a real conjure + graft cloud build.
222	- A single grep per family returns the complete cluster:
223	  `grep -rn rbnve_ Tools/`, `grep -rn rbnne_ Tools/`,
224	  `grep -rn bunne_ Tools/` — and `grep -rn bubep_` returns nothing.
225	
226	## Out of scope
227	
228	- Sprue convention applied to non-regime-enum domains (status codes, JSON
229	  wire-format keys, etc.) — separate heat.
230	- `RBRN_RUNTIME` (`docker`/`podman`) — executable-name coupling, excluded
231	  by decision above.
232	- `BURE_VERBOSE` / `BURE_COLOR` integer values — different problem class.
233	- Backwards-compatibility shims of any kind.
234	
235	## Pace shape (deferred to mount-time)
236	
237	Resolved cadence is per-variable-family, ordered cheap-pilot-first:
238	
239	1. Convention spec authoring — formalize the convention as a spec section,
240	   citing `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding, plus the class-4
241	   "coupled derived string stays bare" rule (two witnesses) and the
242	   `RBRN_RUNTIME` exclusion rationale.
243	2. Egress-mode pilot — `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`. Smallest, cleanest;
244	   proves the mechanical pattern and the class-4 distinction.
245	3. Vessel-mode — `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft`. Heaviest; crosses
246	   bash/adoc/Python(Cloud Build)/Rust. Acceptance gate = real conjure +
247	   graft cloud build.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BM

## Context

Regime enums in this project carry their values as bare English words —
`conjure`, `bind`, `graft` for `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`; `tether`, `airgap` for
`RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`; node-regime modes (`disabled`/`enabled`/`global`/
`allowlist`) for the `RBRN_*` family. These bare values appear scattered
across bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build steps), and Rust (test
fixtures), with no cluster-discoverable marker tying any one value to its
enum family. This makes mode renames cross-language hand-search-and-replace
work, prone to silent drift.

There is one prior in-tree exemplar of the prefixed-value approach:
`bubep_linux | bubep_mac | bubep_windows` for `BURN_PLATFORM`. It works but
uses a different shape from what this heat adopts; harmonization is part of
the sweep.

The inventory pace has been completed (see heat log / slate commit
messages for depth). This paddock records the resolved scope and the
durable findings; line-numbered site lists deliberately live in the
discovery recipe, not here.

## MCM grounding — "sprue" is `mcm_sprue`, prefix discipline makes it an inlay

"Sprue" here is not a coined word — it is the MCM quoin `mcm_sprue`: *a
literal wire-level token representing a serialized name — the exact
character sequence as it appears in a wire format (a JSON property key, an
API field name, a protocol identifier).* Our regime enum values are
textbook sprues: `rbnve_conjure` is the verbatim token that appears in
`rbrv.env`, in the `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` Cloud Build substitution, in
build-info JSON, and in Python/shell comparisons.

MCM also states: *a sprue that follows the prefix naming discipline is also
an `mcm_inlay`* (prefix-recognized, not lexicon-catalogued). So precisely,
the values are *already* sprues even while bare; what this heat adds is the
prefix discipline that makes each sprue **also an inlay**. The spec pace
(₢BMAAA) should cite `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding rather than
inventing rationale.

Note on a sibling usage: ₣BK independently uses "sprue" for tabtarget
dispatch tokens (`{owner}ml_{launcher-id}`, e.g. `rbml_rbw`) and wrote a
local gloss in BCG/CLAUDE.md. That is *also* a valid `mcm_sprue` instance
(a literal dispatch-wire token) — not a competing definition, a sibling
one. Both heats correctly name wire-level literal tokens "sprue." ₣BK's BCG
text would ideally cite `mcm_sprue` rather than read as a freshly-minted
concept, but that is ₣BK's documentation concern, not a blocker here.

## Convention — locked

Pattern: `<proj><n><regime><e>_<value>` where:

- `<proj>` — project prefix (`rb` for Recipe Bottle, `bu` for BUK)
- `<n>`    — single letter signaling "this is a regime namespace value"
             (chosen `n` for "namespace", contrasting `r` which is taken
             for the regime-variable family `rbr*`/`bur*`)
- `<regime>` — single letter mirroring the regime family
             (`v` = vessel, `n` = node, `c` = config, `s` = station,
             `e` = environment, etc.)
- `<e>`    — single letter signaling "this is an enum value"
- `_<value>` — the canonical value name

Full target table (all in-scope enums):

| Variable                 | Regime | Bare → Sprue                                          |
|--------------------------|:------:|-------------------------------------------------------|
| RBRV_VESSEL_MODE         | v      | bind/conjure/graft → `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft` |
| RBRV_EGRESS_MODE         | v      | tether/airgap → `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`              |
| RBRN_ENTRY_MODE          | n      | disabled/enabled → `rbnne_disabled`/`_enabled`        |
| RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE     | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
| RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
| BURN_PLATFORM            | n      | bubep_linux/mac/windows → `bunne_linux`/`_mac`/`_windows` |

Family-sharing is a deliberate property, not an accident: vessel-mode and
egress-mode share `rbnve_` (both vessel-regime); the three node-mode enums
share `rbnne_`, so `rbnne_disabled` is intentionally voiced by ENTRY,
DNS, and ACCESS alike. Uniqueness lives in the full quoin + the owning
variable, not in the 5-char prefix. A single grep per family returns the
whole cluster.

## Scope decisions — resolved

- **RBRV_VESSEL_MODE + RBRV_EGRESS_MODE** → `rbnve_*`. In.
- **RBRN_ENTRY_MODE / UPLINK_DNS_MODE / UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE** → `rbnne_*`.
  In. Their values are pure comparison tokens with no second life, so the
  prefix is free (no indirection needed).
- **BURN_PLATFORM** → harmonize `bubep_*` to `bunne_*` in the same sweep.
  Decided harmonize over grandfather, per load-bearing-complexity
  discipline — eliminates the two-shape wart rather than freezing it.
- **RBRN_RUNTIME (`docker`/`podman`) — EXCLUDED, stays bare.** Its value
  is consumed as the literal executable name (`command -v "${RBRN_RUNTIME}"`,
  `${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect`). Adopting the convention would require a
  permanent value→binary indirection layer at every dispatch site — a
  runtime cost paid for grep-discoverability of two values that
  `RBRN_RUNTIME` itself already marks. The convention would cost more than
  it returns. This is the one regime enum that does not earn the prefix;
  the exclusion is deliberate, not an oversight.
- **BURE_VERBOSE / BURE_COLOR** — out (bare ints, different problem class;
  unchanged from original framing).

## Comment / use-site classification — judgment work

Bare-word grep on a value yields four classes. The migration paces walk
the candidate list and classify each hit:

1. **Phase / concept references** — "the conjure phase", "the airgap
   build" — refer to the workflow, not the enum value. **Leave alone.**
2. **Enum-value references** — equality tests, case arms, JSON literals,
   `vessel mode is conjure`, defaults — the actual variable value.
   **Migrate.**
3. **Identifier embeds** — `RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc`, `rbho_conjure_*`,
   vessel sigils `rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap`, test-step labels like
   `ordain-conjure` — the bare word is part of an identifier. **Leave
   alone.**
4. **Coupled derived strings** — the value drives a derived token that
   lives in resource-name space and must stay bare even though it is
   semantically the enum value. **Leave the derived string bare; migrate
   only the dispatch that selects it.** Two confirmed witnesses:
   - egress → worker-pool suffixes (`RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_*`, the `-tether`/
     `-airgap` fragments in pool paths)
   - vessel-mode → hallmark prefixes (`RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_*` = `c`/`b`/`g`)
   Because of class 4, even a class-2 case arm is not blind find/replace:
   the arm becomes `rbnve_tether)` while the pool string it maps to stays
   `-tether`.

### Three judgment traps a naive grep misses

- **Default-value defaults** — `${z_mode:-conjure}` is class-2 but a
  `== "conjure"` grep skips it. The recipe must also grep `:-conjure`,
  `:-bind`, etc.
- **AsciiDoc wire-value tables read like prose** — a spec table cell
  documenting `(conjure, bind, or graft)` as a substitution value is
  class-2 (migrate), not class-1 prose. Easy to misclassify and leave
  stale.
- **Rust noise is almost all class-3** — the onboarding-scenario fixtures
  are full of step labels (`ordain-conjure`, `wrest-bind`) and sigil
  consts that stay bare. Only the `export RBRV_*_MODE="..."` baseline
  strings in the fast-fixture file actually migrate; that pace rebuilds
  the Rust crate (`tt/vow-b`).

### Noise ranking (classification effort)

Counterintuitively `conjure` is the noisiest value (heavy phase/operation
prose), then `bind` (English-word noise: bind-mount, IP bind), then
`graft`; `tether`/`airgap` are the cleanest (~10 sites each, mostly
class-2 or class-4). This is why egress is the cheap pilot and vessel-mode
is the heavy classification grind.

## Cloud Build detection gap — vessel-mode acceptance gate

Vessel-mode's value crosses a substitution boundary
(`_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` / `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`) into a **remote execution
context** — Cloud Build step shells and Python steps compare against the
literal value. The local `regime-validation` net catches the
declaration↔Rust-fixture lockstep, but it does **not** exercise the
remote-side comparisons. A missed step-shell or Python site fails silently
until an actual conjure/graft cloud build runs.

**Consequence:** for the vessel-mode pace, "gauntlet green" is not
sufficient proof of done. Its acceptance gate is an actual **conjure +
graft cloud build** (the full ordain path), not just the fast/crucible
suites.

## Discovery recipe

- `grep -rn 'buv_enum_enroll' Tools/buk/ Tools/rbk/` — authoritative
  declarations; cross-walk `buv_gate_enroll` invocations to confirm no
  enum missed.
- Per value V: `grep -rn '\bV\b' Tools/ tt/` filtered by extension for
  code sites; also `grep -rn ':-V\b'` for default-value defaults.
- Comment/prose: `grep -rn '#.*\bV\b'` (bash/Python), `'//.*\bV\b'`
  (Rust), and scan `Tools/*/vov_veiled/*.adoc` for prose + wire-value
  tables.
- Substitution boundary: trace `_RBG[AV]_VESSEL_MODE` /
  `_RBG[AV]_EGRESS*` through `rbfd_*`/`rbfv_*` step assembly into the
  `rbgja*`/`rbgjv*` Python+shell steps.

## Posture

**Burn-bridges migration.** No backwards compatibility. Depots reform on
execution, vessels re-charge, regime files get edited in place. Operator
pre-flight: `rbw-MZ` before sweep, all crucibles quenched.

**Deep-test stragglers.** `regime-validation`, `dockerfile-hygiene`, and
`gauntlet` catch any local bare-value site, because the enum-validation
gate rejects a non-prefixed value once the declaration is updated. The
remote Cloud Build comparisons are the exception — see the detection gap
above.

## Heat-shape note — ₣BK relationship (BK coding landed)

₣BK (moorings-cutover) swept essentially this heat's file surface
(`Tools/rbk/**/*.sh`, `rbtd/src/*.rs`, `RBS*.adoc`) — but for path literals,
not enum values. The two heats are **semantically orthogonal** (different
concerns, mostly different lines). ₣BK's *coding* has landed (BKAAI
wrapped); the prior file-surface-churn concern is now historical — the tree
is settled and the re-validation confirmed the enum-value sites survived
intact.

What remains and matters:
- **Start gate:** ₣BK's two-platform gauntlet acceptance is still pending.
  Begin this heat only after ₣BK's gauntlet passes on linux + macos and any
  cutover bugfixes land — so a known-good cutover baseline exists and every
  ₣BM gauntlet signal is unambiguously about enums, not the cutover.
- **Residual churn risk:** only late ₣BK bugfixes from its gauntlet runs.
  Low; re-grep before editing if BK commits land meanwhile.
- The bubep→bunne harmonize touches BUK files (₣BK's other subtree). ₣BK's
  BUK code has landed, so no hard coordination remains — just awareness of
  any late bugfix churn there.

## What done looks like

- All in-scope bare enum values replaced with sprue-prefixed values across
  bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build), and Rust; `RBRN_RUNTIME` left bare.
- `buv_enum_enroll` (and gated `buv_gate_enroll`) declarations updated to
  expect the new values; regime files (`rbrv.env`, `rbrn.env`, BURN
  profiles) updated in place.
- Class-4 derived strings (pool suffixes, hallmark prefixes) left bare;
  their selecting dispatch migrated.
- `regime-validation` + `dockerfile-hygiene` + `gauntlet` green.
- Vessel-mode additionally proven by a real conjure + graft cloud build.
- A single grep per family returns the complete cluster:
  `grep -rn rbnve_ Tools/`, `grep -rn rbnne_ Tools/`,
  `grep -rn bunne_ Tools/` — and `grep -rn bubep_` returns nothing.

## Out of scope

- Sprue convention applied to non-regime-enum domains (status codes, JSON
  wire-format keys, etc.) — separate heat.
- `RBRN_RUNTIME` (`docker`/`podman`) — executable-name coupling, excluded
  by decision above.
- `BURE_VERBOSE` / `BURE_COLOR` integer values — different problem class.
- Backwards-compatibility shims of any kind.

## Pace shape (deferred to mount-time)

Resolved cadence is per-variable-family, ordered cheap-pilot-first:

1. Convention spec authoring — formalize the convention as a spec section,
   citing `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding, plus the class-4
   "coupled derived string stays bare" rule (two witnesses) and the
   `RBRN_RUNTIME` exclusion rationale.
2. Egress-mode pilot — `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`. Smallest, cleanest;
   proves the mechanical pattern and the class-4 distinction.
3. Vessel-mode — `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft`. Heaviest; crosses
   bash/adoc/Python(Cloud Build)/Rust. Acceptance gate = real conjure +
   graft cloud build.
4. Node-mode — `rbnne_*` for ENTRY/DNS/ACCESS. High English-word noise,
   heavy classification.
5. bubep → bunne harmonize — BUK files; last slice.
6. Final regression sweep — gauntlet end-to-end + per-family grep audit.

Slate as thin dockets at mount-time (depth in slate commit messages); do
not pre-cut detailed site lists from this paddock.

# jjezs_pace ₢BMAAG

## Character

Theurge robustness fix; mostly mechanical, with one empirical timing check.

## Problem

canonical-invest reruns against a standing depot fail: `rbgg_invest_retriever`
and `rbgg_invest_director` are create-only and return HTTP 409 when the SA
already exists from a prior run. `governor_mantle` does NOT have this problem —
it deletes existing `governor-*` SAs before recreating ("Clean up existing
governor-* service accounts (404-tolerant)"). Skirmish is designed to rerun
against a standing depot, so this breaks every run after the first.

## Fix

Give the retriever/director invests the same self-heal the mantle already has: a
delete-first-tolerate-404 prelude inside the invest operation. The
GovernorDivests verbs (`rbw-arD` / `rbw-adD`, zero-arg by-role) already exist to
do the deletion. Preferred over a sequence-level divest step in the
canonical-invest fixture, because folding it into the operation also makes
standalone single-case reruns idempotent and co-locates cleanup with creation,
matching the governor precedent.

## Caveat to verify

Tight delete→recreate timing. SA deletion has propagation latency and GCP can
balk at recreating a just-deleted SA name (soft-delete tombstone). Proven this
session that delete-then-recreate works with ~1 min of intervening delay; an
immediate divest→invest is tighter. The mantle already carries a "Wait for SA
propagation" step — the invest self-heal likely needs the same settle. One
tight-timing rerun confirms.

## Done

canonical-invest reruns clean against a standing depot — no 409 — both as the
full skirmish suite and as a standalone single-case rerun.

content.text:
## Mount: ₢BMAAG — idempotent-canonical-invests

**Goal:** Make `canonical-invest` reruns clean against a standing depot — no HTTP 409 when service accounts already exist from a prior run — so skirmish (designed to rerun against a standing depot) survives every run after the first.

**Heat context:** ₣BM `rbk-09-mvp-regime-sprues-misc` (5/8 done). The heat's center of gravity is the *sprue convention* sweep — prefixing bare regime enum values (`conjure`→`rbnve_conjure`, etc.) across bash/adoc/Python/Rust. But ₢BMAAG is an off-axis robustness fix that got slated into this heat: a theurge/governor SA-lifecycle bug, not enum work.

---

**⚠️ Docket/landed-code divergence — needs your call before I touch anything.**

The docket proposes **option B**: fold a delete-first-tolerate-404 prelude *inside* the invest operation (mirror the `governor_mantle` self-heal).

But the most recent ₢BMAAG commit (`e0d8fb281`) explicitly took **option A instead**, and rejected B by name:

> *"Make canonical-invest reruns idempotent via spec-honoring fixture-level divest (option A), **not invest auto-heal**. Honoring RBSRK/RBSDK intent, invest stays fail-loud: added the existence preflight the code never actually implemented (GET before create → `buc_die` 'run divest first to re-key')... The canonical-invest fixture now runs an explicit divest before invest... New `rbgu_poll_until_gone`... Theurge builds clean, 114/114 unit tests, shellcheck clean. **Live skirmish rerun (standing-depot acceptance gate) deferred to operator.**"*

So the code work appears **already landed** — but via the opposite mechanism the docket recommends. The reasoning in that commit is sound (it preserves the RBGG "409-is-fatal" invariant rather than carving it out, and absorbs the deletion-propagation race with a poll-until-404). The divergence reads as deliberate: a better idea found mid-execution, docket never reslated.

**What that leaves for done:** the docket's acceptance criterion — *"reruns clean against a standing depot, both full skirmish suite and standalone single-case rerun"* — is the one piece **not** satisfied. It's the live empirical gate, explicitly deferred to operator. That needs GCP credentials and a standing depot (`service` tier and a live skirmish), which is operator-driven, not something I run unattended.

Let me confirm the tree state matches the commit:

content.text:
Now we have the **real** cause — and it's not in ₢BMAAG's code path at all, nor something a retry will clear:

```
precondition 'canonical depot moniker installed' not met:
RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER="canest3bhm100001" does not begin with 'canest3bhl'
— canonical depot moniker not installed
remediation: rerun rbtdrk_depot_levy or the full canonical-establish fixture
```

The `canonical-invest` fixture has a **precondition gate** that checks the standing depot's moniker prefix. It expects `canest3bhl…` but the installed depot is `canest3bhm…`. The `governor_mantle` case never actually ran — it was gated out before doing any SA work. So we've never reached the retriever/director invest-rerun path that ₢BMAAG fixes.

Let me confirm where that expected prefix comes from so I hand you the right remediation rather than guessing:

content.text:
Found it. The mismatch is a **tincture** mismatch, not a code defect.

The canonical family stem is `canest3` + `BURS_TINCTURE`, where `BURS_TINCTURE` comes from your station file (`burs.env`, via `BURD_STATION_FILE`). The fixture computed expected prefix `canest3bhl…`, so your station is currently configured with `BURS_TINCTURE=bhl`. But the **standing depot** is `canest3bhm100001` — levied under tincture `bhm`.

`rbw-tS` skirmish deliberately *reuses* a standing depot and does no project churn — it assumes a tincture-matching depot already exists. With the station on `bhl` and the only standing depot on `bhm`, there's nothing for it to reuse. That's why all three runs gated out at the same point, and why repair-then-retry didn't move it.

Let me confirm the current station tincture so the picture is exact:

input.command: echo "BURD_STATION_FILE=${BURD_STATION_FILE:-<unset in this shell>}"; F=$(find rbmm_moorings -name "burs.env" 2>/dev/null | head; find . -maxdepth 4 -name "burs.env" 2>/dev/null | head); echo "candidates:"; echo "$F"; for sf in $F; do echo "--- $sf"; grep -n "BURS_TINCTURE" "$sf" 2>/dev/null; done

Now I have the full picture. The skirmish failure is **unrelated to ₢BMAAG**:

```
docker pull failed for …/cancbhm-canest3bhm100001-gar/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire
→ no matching manifest for linux/amd64 in the manifest list entries
```

That's the `onboarding-sequence` fixture failing to pull the **standing depot's RBRD tripwire image** — it has no `linux/amd64` manifest. A property of this particular standing depot's inscribed tripwire, not a code defect and not anything ₢BMAAG touched.

## Result for ₢BMAAG: verified ✓

The pace's acceptance path passed cleanly against the standing depot:

| canonical-invest case | result | significance |
|---|---|---|
| `rbtdrk_governor_mantle` | PASSED | depot/governor preflight |
| `rbtdrk_retriever_invest` | **PASSED** | the rerun-against-existing-SA path that used to 409 |
| `rbtdrk_director_invest` | **PASSED** | same |

These ran against service accounts that already existed from prior runs (skirmish reuses the standing depot) — the exact no-409 rerun scenario ₢BMAAG fixes, and the tight delete→recreate timing the docket flagged. The fixture-level divest + `rbgu_poll_until_gone` did their job. **3/3, no 409.**

The docket's "Done" has two clauses: *full skirmish suite* (the canonical-invest portion passed clean — the downstream suite failure is the unrelated tripwire issue) and *standalone single-case rerun* (not yet run separately).

content.text:
Root cause found. The tripwire image in the standing depot is **single-arch arm64** — its manifest list has only `arm64/linux` plus an `unknown/unknown` attestation blob. **No `linux/amd64`.** The inscribe-reliquary step runs the pull on an amd64 host, so it fails.

This points at how the tripwire was inscribed. Let me confirm the normal build/push path to state the remediation precisely:

content.text:
The investigation is conclusive. Here's the mechanism:

**`rbrd_inscribe`** (rbndb_base.sh:142) builds the tripwire with a bare `docker build` from a `FROM scratch` Dockerfile — **no `--platform` flag**, so it bakes the *inscriber's native architecture*. Then `rbrd_check`/inscribe-reliquary's pre-pull (rbndb_base.sh:197) does `docker pull` on the *consuming* host.

The standing depot `canest3bhm100001`'s tripwire is **arm64-only** → it was levied/inscribed from an **arm64 host (a Mac)**. This Linux test station is **amd64**, and Cloud Build is amd64 — neither can pull an arm64-only manifest. Hence "no matching manifest for linux/amd64."

The irony: the tripwire is `FROM scratch` carrying only `rbrd.env` — pure data, architecture-irrelevant — but Docker's manifest-list platform selection still demands a matching platform on pull. So a Mac-inscribed depot produces a tripwire that no amd64 consumer (including Cloud Build) can ever pull. That's a **latent cross-arch depot-portability bug in `rbrd_inscribe`**, independent of ₢BMAAG.

Let me check the standalone retriever_invest result:

content.text:
Definitive proof — the standalone rerun exercised ₢BMAAG's full mechanism end to end:

1. **`rbgg_divest_retriever`** divested the *existing* SA (`retriever-canest-ret@cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001…`) and removed its installed RBRA — confirming this was a real rerun against a pre-existing SA, not a no-op.
2. **`rbgg_invest_retriever`** then ran the **fail-loud existence preflight** ₢BMAAG added (*"Preflight: confirm retriever-canest-ret does not already exist"*) — which passed *because* the divest's `poll_until_gone` had already confirmed deletion propagated — then **"Service account created"** with **no 409**.

That's the exact delete→propagate→recreate chain the docket's caveat flagged as the risk, holding under tight timing. Confirmed **twice** now (in-skirmish + immediate standalone).

---

## ₢BMAAG — fully verified ✓

| Docket "Done" clause | Evidence |
|---|---|
| Reruns clean as full skirmish suite | canonical-invest 3/3 against standing depot, no 409 |
| Reruns clean as standalone single-case | `retriever_invest` 1/1, divest→poll→recreate, no 409 |

Code was already landed (option A, 114/114 unit). The live standing-depot acceptance gate — the one thing that was operator-deferred — is now **met empirically**.

## Tripwire bug (investigation complete, unrelated to ₢BMAAG)

`rbrd_inscribe` (rbndb_base.sh:142) builds the `FROM scratch` tripwire with no `--platform`, baking the inscriber's native arch. The `bhm` depot was inscribed from arm64 (a Mac); this station and Cloud Build are amd64, so the pull fails. The tripwire is immutable (rbndb_base.sh:127 refuses re-inscribe over it), so refreshing it requires unmake + re-levy. The durable fix is making `rbrd_inscribe` force `linux/amd64` (or multi-arch) since the image is pure data and Cloud Build consumers are always amd64.

Two decisions:

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# shellcheck disable=SC2153  # kindle chain - per BCG
3	#
4	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
5	#
6	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
7	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
8	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
9	#
10	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
11	#
12	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
13	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
14	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
15	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
16	# limitations under the License.
17	#
18	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
19	#
20	# Recipe Bottle Depot Regime Bespoke - Tripwire helpers
21	#
22	# Implements the depot-immutable tripwire. At end of successful levy
23	# the Payor inscribes a FROM-scratch image carrying rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env to
24	# GAR at rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire. Every cloud-submitting command pulls
25	# that image and exact-byte-diffs the file before submitting, so any
26	# post-levy hand-edit of rbrd.env dies loudly instead of silently
27	# differing from the depot's worker-pool quotas, region, and identity.
28	#
29	# Public functions:
30	#   rbrd_inscribe <bearer_token>   — host-side push at end of levy
31	#   rbrd_check    <bearer_token>   — pre-submit existence + byte-diff
32	#
33	# Module convention: rbn{R}{X}_ where R=d (depot regime) and X=b
34	# (bespoke implementation). See CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline".
35	
36	set -euo pipefail
37	
38	# Multiple inclusion detection
39	test -z "${ZRBNDB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbndb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
40	ZRBNDB_SOURCED=1
41	
42	######################################################################
43	# Internal Functions (zrbndb_*)
44	
45	zrbndb_kindle() {
46	  test -z "${ZRBNDB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbndb already kindled"
47	
48	  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
49	  zrbgc_sentinel
50	  zrbgl_sentinel
51	  zrbrd_sentinel
52	  zrbdc_sentinel
53	
54	  # Tripwire image FQN composed once at kindle time from depot's GAR
55	  # coordinates + the rbi_df depot-facts namespace.
56	  # Shape: <region>-docker.pkg.dev/<depot-project>/<gar-repo>/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire
57	  readonly ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBRD_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
58	  readonly ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE="${ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT}/rbrd:tripwire"
59	
60	  # Per-command temp prefixes (forensic-preserving — files persist after run)
61	  readonly ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbndb_inscribe_"
62	  readonly ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbndb_check_"
63	
64	  readonly ZRBNDB_KINDLED=1
65	}
66	
67	zrbndb_sentinel() {
68	  test "${ZRBNDB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbndb not kindled - call zrbndb_kindle first"
69	}
70	
71	# Authenticate the host docker client to the depot's GAR registry
72	# using a bearer token. Idempotent — repeated calls refresh credential.
73	# Args: bearer_token stderr_file
74	zrbndb_docker_login() {
75	  zrbndb_sentinel
76	
77	  local -r z_token="${1}"
78	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2}"
79	
80	  printf '%s' "${z_token}" \
81	    | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "${ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
82	        > /dev/null 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
83	    || buc_die "Docker login to ${ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST} failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"
84	}
85	
86	######################################################################
87	# External Functions (rbrd_*)
88	
89	# Inscribe the tripwire image at end of successful levy.
90	# Pre-push existence guard: image present → fatal (depot already
91	# inscribed; must be unmade and relevied to refresh).
92	# Token sources (in order): positional $1, then BUZ_FOLIO (tabtarget
93	# param1 channel). The former is the consuming-CLI path (caller has a
94	# token in scope); the latter is the rbw-rdi tabtarget path.
95	rbrd_inscribe() {
96	  zrbndb_sentinel
97	
98	  buc_doc_brief "Inscribe RBRD tripwire image to GAR (host-side, at end of levy)"
99	  buc_doc_param "bearer_token" "OAuth access token with GAR write (Payor)"
100	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
101	
102	  local -r z_token="${1:-${BUZ_FOLIO:-}}"
103	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "rbrd_inscribe: bearer token required (positional arg or BUZ_FOLIO)"
104	
105	  local -r z_login_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}login_stderr.txt"
106	  local -r z_manifest_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}manifest_stderr.txt"
107	  local -r z_build_dir="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}build"
108	  local -r z_dockerfile="${z_build_dir}/Dockerfile"
109	  local -r z_rbrd_copy="${z_build_dir}/rbrd.env"
110	  local -r z_build_stdout="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}build_stdout.txt"
111	  local -r z_build_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}build_stderr.txt"
112	  local -r z_push_stdout="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}push_stdout.txt"
113	  local -r z_push_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}push_stderr.txt"
114	
115	  buc_step "Inscribe RBRD tripwire image to GAR"
116	  buc_log_args "Target: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
117	
118	  zrbndb_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_login_stderr}"
119	
120	  buc_log_args "Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect"
121	  if docker manifest inspect "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_manifest_stderr}"; then
122	    buc_warn "Tripwire image already inscribed: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
123	    buc_info "RBRD is depot-time-immutable. To refresh the tripwire, the depot"
124	    buc_info "must be unmade and relevied:"
125	    buc_info "  rbw-dU \$(rbw-dl)        # unmake the depot"
126	    buc_info "  rbw-dL                  # relevy with new rbrd.env"
127	    buc_die "Cannot inscribe over existing tripwire (would mask drift)"
128	  fi
129	
130	  buc_log_args "Build FROM-scratch image carrying ${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
131	  mkdir -p "${z_build_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create build dir ${z_build_dir}"
132	
133	  cp "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" "${z_rbrd_copy}" \
134	    || buc_die "Failed to copy ${RBCC_rbrd_file} into build context"
135	
136	  printf '%s\n' \
137	    'FROM scratch' \
138	    'COPY rbrd.env /rbrd.env' \
139	    > "${z_dockerfile}" \
140	    || buc_die "Failed to write Dockerfile at ${z_dockerfile}"
141	
142	  docker build -t "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" "${z_build_dir}" \
143	      > "${z_build_stdout}" 2>"${z_build_stderr}" \
144	    || buc_die "docker build failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_build_stderr}"
145	
146	  buc_log_args "Push tripwire image to GAR"
147	  docker push "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" \
148	      > "${z_push_stdout}" 2>"${z_push_stderr}" \
149	    || buc_die "docker push failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_push_stderr}"
150	
151	  buc_success "Tripwire inscribed: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
152	}
153	
154	# Check local rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env against the inscribed tripwire image
155	# before submitting cloud work. Exact-byte mismatch, missing image,
156	# or registry/auth failure all fatal with recovery guidance.
157	# Token sources mirror rbrd_inscribe: positional $1 then BUZ_FOLIO.
158	rbrd_check() {
159	  zrbndb_sentinel
160	
161	  buc_doc_brief "Check local rbrd.env against inscribed tripwire image (drift detector)"
162	  buc_doc_param "bearer_token" "OAuth access token with GAR read (any role: Payor, Director, Retriever)"
163	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
164	
165	  local -r z_token="${1:-${BUZ_FOLIO:-}}"
166	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "rbrd_check: bearer token required (positional arg or BUZ_FOLIO)"
167	
168	  local -r z_login_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}login_stderr.txt"
169	  local -r z_manifest_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}manifest_stderr.txt"
170	  local -r z_pull_stdout="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}pull_stdout.txt"
171	  local -r z_pull_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}pull_stderr.txt"
172	  local -r z_create_stdout="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}create_stdout.txt"
173	  local -r z_create_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}create_stderr.txt"
174	  local -r z_cp_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}cp_stderr.txt"
175	  local -r z_rm_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}rm_stderr.txt"
176	  local -r z_inscribed_file="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}rbrd.env.inscribed"
177	  local -r z_diff_file="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}diff.txt"
178	  local -r z_diff_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}diff_stderr.txt"
179	
180	  buc_log_args "Tripwire check against: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
181	
182	  zrbndb_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_login_stderr}"
183	
184	  buc_log_args "Existence check via docker manifest inspect"
185	  docker manifest inspect "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_manifest_stderr}" \
186	    || {
187	      buc_warn "Tripwire image absent or unreachable: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
188	      buc_info "Manifest-inspect stderr: ${z_manifest_stderr}"
189	      buc_info "If depot has not been inscribed, or the tripwire was jettisoned,"
190	      buc_info "re-levy the depot to mint the tripwire:"
191	      buc_info "  rbw-dU \$(rbw-dl)        # unmake (if a stale depot exists)"
192	      buc_info "  rbw-dL                  # levy + inscribe tripwire"
193	      buc_die "Cannot proceed without tripwire — depot regime drift cannot be detected"
194	    }
195	
196	  buc_log_args "Pull tripwire image"
197	  docker pull "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" \
198	      > "${z_pull_stdout}" 2>"${z_pull_stderr}" \
199	    || buc_die "docker pull failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_pull_stderr}"
200	
201	  buc_log_args "Create temp container to extract /rbrd.env"
202	  # The tripwire is FROM-scratch (no CMD/ENTRYPOINT); the Linux daemon rejects
203	  # docker create on such an image with "no command specified". The container
204	  # is never started — only docker cp + docker rm touch it — so a never-executed
205	  # placeholder command satisfies the create-time requirement harmlessly.
206	  docker create "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" /rbrd.env \
207	      > "${z_create_stdout}" 2>"${z_create_stderr}" \
208	    || buc_die "docker create failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_create_stderr}"
209	
210	  local -r z_cid=$(<"${z_create_stdout}")
211	  test -n "${z_cid}" || buc_die "docker create returned empty container ID — see ${z_create_stdout}"
212	
213	  docker cp "${z_cid}:/rbrd.env" "${z_inscribed_file}" 2>"${z_cp_stderr}" \
214	    || {
215	      docker rm "${z_cid}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_rm_stderr}" \
216	        || buc_warn "Failed to remove temp container ${z_cid} — see ${z_rm_stderr}"
217	      buc_die "docker cp failed extracting /rbrd.env from tripwire image — see ${z_cp_stderr}"
218	    }
219	
220	  docker rm "${z_cid}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_rm_stderr}" \
221	    || buc_warn "Failed to remove temp container ${z_cid} — see ${z_rm_stderr}"
222	
223	  # cmp is authoritative for byte-match; diff is for human display on mismatch.
224	  # diff exits 1 when differences exist — expected since cmp already detected
225	  # them. Exit ≥ 2 is a real diff error; capture explicit status and fatal.
226	  if ! cmp -s "${z_inscribed_file}" "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"; then
227	    local z_diff_status=0
228	    diff -u "${z_inscribed_file}" "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" \
229	        > "${z_diff_file}" 2>"${z_diff_stderr}" \
230	      || z_diff_status=$?
231	    test "${z_diff_status}" -le 1 \
232	      || buc_die "diff failed (status ${z_diff_status}) generating drift report — see ${z_diff_stderr}"
233	    buc_warn "RBRD drift detected"
234	    buc_info "Local ${RBCC_rbrd_file} differs from the depot-inscribed copy."
235	    buc_info "Full diff (inscribed → local) at: ${z_diff_file}"
236	    buc_info "Recovery options:"
237	    buc_info "  (a) Restore ${RBCC_rbrd_file} to match the inscribed copy (preserves depot)."
238	    buc_info "  (b) Unmake and re-levy the depot if rbrd.env genuinely needs to change:"
239	    buc_info "        rbw-dU \$(rbw-dl)"
240	    buc_info "        rbw-dL"
241	    buc_die "Refusing to submit cloud work against drifted depot regime"
242	  fi
243	
244	  buc_log_args "Tripwire match — depot regime aligned"
245	}
246	
247	# eof
248

#

# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Depot Regime Bespoke - Tripwire helpers
#
# Implements the depot-immutable tripwire. At end of successful levy
# the Payor inscribes a FROM-scratch image carrying rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env to
# GAR at rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire. Every cloud-submitting command pulls
# that image and exact-byte-diffs the file before submitting, so any
# post-levy hand-edit of rbrd.env dies loudly instead of silently
# differing from the depot's worker-pool quotas, region, and identity.
#
# Public functions:
#   rbrd_inscribe <bearer_token>   — host-side push at end of levy
#   rbrd_check    <bearer_token>   — pre-submit existence + byte-diff
#
# Module convention: rbn{R}{X}_ where R=d (depot regime) and X=b
# (bespoke implementation). See CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline".

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBNDB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbndb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBNDB_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbndb_*)

zrbndb_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBNDB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbndb already kindled"

  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
  zrbgc_sentinel
  zrbgl_sentinel
  zrbrd_sentinel
  zrbdc_sentinel

  # Tripwire image FQN composed once at kindle time from depot's GAR
  # coordinates + the rbi_df depot-facts namespace.
  # Shape: <region>-docker.pkg.dev/<depot-project>/<gar-repo>/rbi_df/rbrd:tripwire
  readonly ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBRD_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  readonly ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE="${ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT}/rbrd:tripwire"

  # Per-command temp prefixes (forensic-preserving — files persist after run)
  readonly ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbndb_inscribe_"
  readonly ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbndb_check_"

  readonly ZRBNDB_KINDLED=1
}

zrbndb_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBNDB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbndb not kindled - call zrbndb_kindle first"
}

# Authenticate the host docker client to the depot's GAR registry
# using a bearer token. Idempotent — repeated calls refresh credential.
# Args: bearer_token stderr_file
zrbndb_docker_login() {
  zrbndb_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2}"

  printf '%s' "${z_token}" \
    | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "${ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
        > /dev/null 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
    || buc_die "Docker login to ${ZRBNDB_REGISTRY_HOST} failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbrd_*)

# Inscribe the tripwire image at end of successful levy.
# Pre-push existence guard: image present → fatal (depot already
# inscribed; must be unmade and relevied to refresh).
# Token sources (in order): positional $1, then BUZ_FOLIO (tabtarget
# param1 channel). The former is the consuming-CLI path (caller has a
# token in scope); the latter is the rbw-rdi tabtarget path.
rbrd_inscribe() {
  zrbndb_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Inscribe RBRD tripwire image to GAR (host-side, at end of levy)"
  buc_doc_param "bearer_token" "OAuth access token with GAR write (Payor)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_token="${1:-${BUZ_FOLIO:-}}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "rbrd_inscribe: bearer token required (positional arg or BUZ_FOLIO)"

  local -r z_login_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}login_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_manifest_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}manifest_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_build_dir="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}build"
  local -r z_dockerfile="${z_build_dir}/Dockerfile"
  local -r z_rbrd_copy="${z_build_dir}/rbrd.env"
  local -r z_build_stdout="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}build_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_build_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}build_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_push_stdout="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}push_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_push_stderr="${ZRBNDB_INSCRIBE_PREFIX}push_stderr.txt"

  buc_step "Inscribe RBRD tripwire image to GAR"
  buc_log_args "Target: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"

  zrbndb_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_login_stderr}"

  buc_log_args "Pre-push existence guard via docker manifest inspect"
  if docker manifest inspect "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_manifest_stderr}"; then
    buc_warn "Tripwire image already inscribed: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
    buc_info "RBRD is depot-time-immutable. To refresh the tripwire, the depot"
    buc_info "must be unmade and relevied:"
    buc_info "  rbw-dU \$(rbw-dl)        # unmake the depot"
    buc_info "  rbw-dL                  # relevy with new rbrd.env"
    buc_die "Cannot inscribe over existing tripwire (would mask drift)"
  fi

  buc_log_args "Build FROM-scratch image carrying ${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
  mkdir -p "${z_build_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create build dir ${z_build_dir}"

  cp "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" "${z_rbrd_copy}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to copy ${RBCC_rbrd_file} into build context"

  printf '%s\n' \
    'FROM scratch' \
    'COPY rbrd.env /rbrd.env' \
    > "${z_dockerfile}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write Dockerfile at ${z_dockerfile}"

  docker build -t "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" "${z_build_dir}" \
      > "${z_build_stdout}" 2>"${z_build_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "docker build failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_build_stderr}"

  buc_log_args "Push tripwire image to GAR"
  docker push "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" \
      > "${z_push_stdout}" 2>"${z_push_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "docker push failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_push_stderr}"

  buc_success "Tripwire inscribed: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
}

# Check local rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env against the inscribed tripwire image
# before submitting cloud work. Exact-byte mismatch, missing image,
# or registry/auth failure all fatal with recovery guidance.
# Token sources mirror rbrd_inscribe: positional $1 then BUZ_FOLIO.
rbrd_check() {
  zrbndb_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check local rbrd.env against inscribed tripwire image (drift detector)"
  buc_doc_param "bearer_token" "OAuth access token with GAR read (any role: Payor, Director, Retriever)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_token="${1:-${BUZ_FOLIO:-}}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "rbrd_check: bearer token required (positional arg or BUZ_FOLIO)"

  local -r z_login_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}login_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_manifest_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}manifest_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_pull_stdout="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}pull_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_pull_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}pull_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_create_stdout="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}create_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_create_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}create_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_cp_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}cp_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_rm_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}rm_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_inscribed_file="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}rbrd.env.inscribed"
  local -r z_diff_file="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}diff.txt"
  local -r z_diff_stderr="${ZRBNDB_CHECK_PREFIX}diff_stderr.txt"

  buc_log_args "Tripwire check against: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"

  zrbndb_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_login_stderr}"

  buc_log_args "Existence check via docker manifest inspect"
  docker manifest inspect "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_manifest_stderr}" \
    || {
      buc_warn "Tripwire image absent or unreachable: ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}"
      buc_info "Manifest-inspect stderr: ${z_manifest_stderr}"
      buc_info "If depot has not been inscribed, or the tripwire was jettisoned,"
      buc_info "re-levy the depot to mint the tripwire:"
      buc_info "  rbw-dU \$(rbw-dl)        # unmake (if a stale depot exists)"
      buc_info "  rbw-dL                  # levy + inscribe tripwire"
      buc_die "Cannot proceed without tripwire — depot regime drift cannot be detected"
    }

  buc_log_args "Pull tripwire image"
  docker pull "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" \
      > "${z_pull_stdout}" 2>"${z_pull_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "docker pull failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_pull_stderr}"

  buc_log_args "Create temp container to extract /rbrd.env"
  # The tripwire is FROM-scratch (no CMD/ENTRYPOINT); the Linux daemon rejects
  # docker create on such an image with "no command specified". The container
  # is never started — only docker cp + docker rm touch it — so a never-executed
  # placeholder command satisfies the create-time requirement harmlessly.
  docker create "${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE}" /rbrd.env \
      > "${z_create_stdout}" 2>"${z_create_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "docker create failed for ${ZRBNDB_TRIPWIRE_IMAGE} — see ${z_create_stderr}"

  local -r z_cid=$(<"${z_create_stdout}")
  test -n "${z_cid}" || buc_die "docker create returned empty container ID — see ${z_create_stdout}"

  docker cp "${z_cid}:/rbrd.env" "${z_inscribed_file}" 2>"${z_cp_stderr}" \
    || {
      docker rm "${z_cid}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_rm_stderr}" \
        || buc_warn "Failed to remove temp container ${z_cid} — see ${z_rm_stderr}"
      buc_die "docker cp failed extracting /rbrd.env from tripwire image — see ${z_cp_stderr}"
    }

  docker rm "${z_cid}" > /dev/null 2>"${z_rm_stderr}" \
    || buc_warn "Failed to remove temp container ${z_cid} — see ${z_rm_stderr}"

  # cmp is authoritative for byte-match; diff is for human display on mismatch.
  # diff exits 1 when differences exist — expected since cmp already detected
  # them. Exit ≥ 2 is a real diff error; capture explicit status and fatal.
  if ! cmp -s "${z_inscribed_file}" "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"; then
    local z_diff_status=0
    diff -u "${z_inscribed_file}" "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" \
        > "${z_diff_file}" 2>"${z_diff_stderr}" \
      || z_diff_status=$?
    test "${z_diff_status}" -le 1 \
      || buc_die "diff failed (status ${z_diff_status}) generating drift report — see ${z_diff_stderr}"
    buc_warn "RBRD drift detected"
    buc_info "Local ${RBCC_rbrd_file} differs from the depot-inscribed copy."
    buc_info "Full diff (inscribed → local) at: ${z_diff_file}"
    buc_info "Recovery options:"
    buc_info "  (a) Restore ${RBCC_rbrd_file} to match the inscribed copy (preserves depot)."
    buc_info "  (b) Unmake and re-levy the depot if rbrd.env genuinely needs to change:"
    buc_info "        rbw-dU \$(rbw-dl)"
    buc_info "        rbw-dL"
    buc_die "Refusing to submit cloud work against drifted depot regime"
  fi

  buc_log_args "Tripwire match — depot regime aligned"
}

# eof
